title: Design node properties hook with undo support
purpose: Manages property values for selected design nodes and provides functions to update them with undo/redo capabilities. Used by the PropertiesPanel to synchronize UI with scene state.

methods.name: useDesignNodeProps
methods.normalized_params: (): { node: SceneNode | null; nodes: SceneNode[]; updateProp: (key: string, value: number | string | boolean) => void; commitProp: (key: string) => void; updateNodeWithUndo: (key: string, value: number | string | boolean, label?: string) => void }

steps.content: Subscribe to selected node IDs and scene version from the design editor store.
Initialize a ref to track original property values before editing begins.
Clear original values when the selection changes to prevent stale data.
Derive the list of selected nodes from the store, updating when selection or scene version changes.
Determine if exactly one node is selected for single-node operations.
Return an object containing the selected node, all selected nodes, and functions to update properties with undo support.

methods.name: updateProp
methods.normalized_params: (key: string, value: number | string | boolean): void

steps.content: Get the current store state and editor references.
For each selected node, capture the original property value if not already recorded.
Update the property value for all selected nodes.
If only one node is selected, update that specific node directly.
Get the current store state and selected nodes.
For each selected node, commit the property update using the captured original value as the previous state.
Clear the original values for the committed property from the tracking ref.

methods.name: updateNodeWithUndo
methods.normalized_params: (key: string, value: number | string | boolean, label?: string): void

steps.content: Get the current store state and selected nodes.
For each selected node, update the property with undo support using the provided label or a default label.
If only one node is selected, update that specific node directly.
